description_long: This bulletin board features a large, rectangular display area divided into two tan-colored panels, framed by a sturdy black metal structure. The frame is reinforced with two vertical posts topped with red spherical caps, adding a touch of visibility and safety. Below the display area, there is a distinctive gray, stepped or serrated panel, likely designed for additional notices or as a protective element. The back and sides of the board reveal a slightly arched, ribbed structure, suggesting durability and weather resistance, suitable for outdoor use. The overall construction appears robust, with the frame and panels securely joined, and the design is both functional and visually clear for public information display. The materials and build indicate it is intended for high-traffic urban environments such as parks, sidewalks, or community centers.
description: A robust, dual-panel bulletin board with a black metal frame, tan display surfaces, and a gray stepped lower section, topped with red safety caps.
description_view_0: Side view showing the arched, ribbed structure and the vertical post with a red cap.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the two tan panels, black frame, and the gray stepped lower section.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring the arched, ribbed structure and vertical post with red cap.
description_view_3: Rear view highlighting the back of the tan panels, black frame, and the full width of the gray stepped section.
attribute_frame_design: Rectangular black metal frame with two vertical posts, moderate thickness, topped with red spherical caps, and a lower gray stepped panel.
